items.summary: Keep ContextLattice native memory plugin and provider discovery in the Rust agent runtime.
items.rationale: This is an intentional Rust-native enhancement that improves memory orchestration and does not replace or remove upstream behavior.

items.summary: Preserve webhook-driven sync queue worker architecture with sqlite, SQS, and Kafka support.
items.rationale: Queue backend plurality (sqlite/SQS/Kafka) is an ultra reliability extension for upstream sync maintenance and does not reduce upstream parity scope.

items.summary: Preserve launchd-based interactive dev lifecycle management for webhook listener and worker.
items.rationale: launchd lifecycle is a macOS operational hardening layer for development upkeep and is intentionally additive.

items.summary: Track upstream skills and optional-skills catalogs via parity audits while keeping Rust runtime skill loading externalized (no direct Python skill-tree vendoring).
items.rationale: Skills parity is delivered by catalog governance and runtime contract equivalence rather than vendoring Python implementation trees in a Rust-first repo.

items.summary: Keep platform adapter parity in the Rust gateway instead of vendoring upstream Python platform-plugin trees.
items.rationale: Mattermost and ntfy platform behavior are represented by Rust-native gateway adapters and adapter contracts; upstream Python plugin files are not vendored into the Rust runtime path.

items.summary: Track newly added upstream Python plugin/backend trees separately from surgical Rust issue-triage fixes.
items.rationale: dashboard_auth/nous, image_gen/krea, and security-guidance were added upstream after this PR branch started. Importing or porting those Python plugin trees is unrelated to the focused #292/#293/#317 fixes and should be handled in a dedicated plugin parity slice.

items.summary: Do not import upstream implementation-plan archive documents unless they change local runtime or release behavior.
items.rationale: The upstream s6-overlay plan is a post-implementation reference document for upstream Docker/Python service-management history; Hermes Ultra tracks relevant runtime parity through local Docker, gateway, and release gates.

items.summary: Treat Rust CLI/TUI and gateway as primary UX surface; upstream web/ui-tui trees are tracked as intentional divergence unless explicitly ported.
items.rationale: User-facing runtime parity is provided via Rust CLI/TUI/gateway. Web/ui-tui trees remain tracked and auditable, but not auto-vendored.
